EY presenting price Wed AM 3.31.26 KK - interested in demo and POC of spend analysis 3.17.26 KK - fu with EY 3.3.26 KK - argos looking for more information on what a POC would look like - connecting internally 2.27.26 KK - FU with EY on any updates 2.18.26 KK - EY shared data request for implementation and SaaS pricing to grupo argos. Waiting for inputs
hard.ais_why:
• Concrete AI-product buyer interest sits in the next-step note from 3/31/26: "interested in demo and POC of spend analysis". A spend-analysis POC aligns to ML iAnalyze and Merlin analytics. This is the strongest buyer-stated AI signal in this part of the cohort after Teads. The deal is advancing through commercials with four pricing scenarios shared and a business case in progress.
• Products are the broadest in this cohort - Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA, iLogix, iCompliance and ML iAnalyze. But AI importance is 'Neutral', which signals Zycus does not perceive the buyer as AI-driven. An EY-led evaluation, multi-pricing scenarios and a structured business case look more like procurement-rigor buying than AI-vision buying. The POC scope is spend analysis - a discrete use case, not Agentic or Intake.
• Avoma was fully exhausted - zero calls at opportunity or account level, no Grupo Argos contacts in OpportunityContactRole (only EY), and zero on grupoargos.com. The deal is EY-led entirely. The spend-analysis POC quote is real but limited in scope, which holds this at Curious.

forecast_read.reason: Pipeline is not defensible on a deal with zero direct Grupo Argos engagement in 175 days, no mapped buyer stakeholders, and the partner (EY) reporting on 29 Jun 2026 that an Argos leadership change plus the recent election have slowed everything. This is a deal-validation problem, not just a date problem: nothing confirms the S2P initiative is still live and funded under the new leadership. Move to Omitted until EY or Argos re-validates the initiative, then re-enter Pipeline with a dated plan.
forecast_read.math: 175 days to close (2026-12-31). Created 15 Jan 2026 (175 days old); in Formal Evaluation 142 days (since 17 Feb 2026 stage move) with no motion toward Shortlisted. Close date has never slipped, but only because nothing is moving it. Real forward motion stopped after commercials: four pricing scenarios shared 24 Apr, amount raised $100k to $350k on 8 May, business case work through 26 May, then 'no update' 16 Jun and leadership-change/election slowdown 29 Jun. To close 31 Dec the buyer still needs demo/POC, shortlist, selection, and contracting; that pace is impossible without buyer re-engagement within weeks.

metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem or ROI case is documented from Grupo Argos. The only value signals are partner-mediated: Argos (via EY) asked what a POC would look like on 3 Mar 2026 and was 'interested in demo and POC of spend analysis' on 31 Mar 2026 (Next Step), and Karson Keogh built a business case with the EY team and Ronit through 12-26 May 2026 (Next Step). No Argos executive has voiced a savings target, cost problem, or cycle-time goal, so the business case rests on EY's framing, not buyer commitment. The move is to extract the financial drivers of the S2P initiative from whoever owns it under Argos's new leadership.

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer is identified. OpportunityContactRole holds zero Grupo Argos people, EY has never named the Argos executive who controls the $350k decision, and the leadership change EY reported on 29 Jun 2026 (Next Step) means any implicit sponsor may now be out of role. EY presented price to Argos around 20 Apr 2026 (Next Step) but never disclosed to whom. Until EY names the budget owner under the new leadership, budget commitment cannot be validated.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are inferred, not buyer-confirmed. The evaluated scope is the full S2P suite plus AI (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, iRequest, Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, iLogix, iRisk, iCompliance, ANA per Products__c), EY requested implementation data and SaaS pricing on 18 Feb 2026, and Argos asked for POC shape (3 Mar) and spend-analysis demo/POC (31 Mar) (Next Step). That implies cost, implementation complexity, and spend-visibility value are the working criteria, but no RFP, scoring matrix, or buyer-stated criteria exist. The move is to get the evaluation framework from EY or Argos directly.

decision_process.narrative: No decision process is documented. EY intermediates everything (pricing 18 Feb-20 Apr, business case May per Next Step) but has never disclosed Argos's evaluation stages, governance, or approval chain, and there is no RFP timeline. As of 29 Jun 2026 EY says a leadership change and the recent election have slowed things (Next Step), so whatever process existed may be paused or under review. Whether the initiative survives the new leadership is the open question that defines this deal.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics are documented: no MSA, SOW, legal, or InfoSec discussion has surfaced anywhere. EY's role as implementation partner is implied by its ownership of pricing and the business case (Feb-May 2026, Next Step), but whether EY or Argos papers the deal is unknown. With the deal at Formal Evaluation and the buyer quiet, this is an expected gap rather than a current blocker; it becomes real once the initiative is re-validated.

identify_pain.narrative: No Argos-articulated pain exists on the record. The full-suite scope and EY's spend-analysis POC ask (31 Mar 2026, Next Step) imply spend-visibility and procurement-efficiency drivers, but that is inferred from the partner's questions, not confirmed by a named Argos pain owner, and there is no compelling event beyond a distant 31 Dec close date. The election-driven slowdown (29 Jun 2026, Next Step) suggests the pain is not currently urgent to Argos leadership. The move is to tie the S2P case to something the new leadership measurably cares about.

champion.narrative: No champion exists. Zero Grupo Argos contacts are mapped, and no Argos advocate has been named in 175 days. EY carried the deal from active (pricing scenarios 24 Apr, business case 12-26 May, Next Step) to passive ('no update' 16 Jun) to reporting external blockers (leadership change and election, 29 Jun). EY's inability to provide stakeholder introductions or a timeline, especially post-leadership-change, suggests limited current influence inside Argos. The deal is single-threaded through a partner who is not driving.

competition.narrative: No named vendor rival appears in any field or note across the deal's life; the reconciled competitive threat is Do Nothing. EY's report of an Argos leadership change and election slowdown (29 Jun 2026, Next Step), plus zero direct buyer engagement in 175 days, means the real fight is against deprioritization and inertia under new leadership. The wedge is the cost of inaction framed for the incoming leaders: spend visibility, compliance, and procurement efficiency across a full S2P scope EY has already priced.
sources: engagement pulse: no buyer activity since creation 2026-01-15

critical_signals.text: EY reported on 29 Jun 2026 that Grupo Argos had a leadership change and that the recent election has slowed everything, putting the whole S2P initiative in question until it is re-validated with the new leaders.
After 175 days there is still not one Grupo Argos stakeholder in the deal; every touch runs through EY, who had no update on 16 Jun 2026 and cannot currently provide access or a timeline.

Commercial groundwork is genuinely done: four pricing scenarios went to EY on 24 Apr 2026, the deal value rose to $350k on 8 May, and a business case was built with EY through 26 May, but Argos has not responded to any of it.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A partner-led S2P evaluation that did real commercial work through May 2026 (four pricing scenarios 24 Apr, amount to $350k 8 May, business case with EY through 26 May) and then lost its buyer: EY had no update 16 Jun and on 29 Jun cited an Argos leadership change plus election slowdown. No Argos person has ever engaged directly, no stakeholder is mapped, and every MEDDPICC fundamental except competition is a gap. The deal is alive on paper and dark in reality.

win_position.text: No Grupo Argos stakeholder has ever engaged directly in 175 days: zero contact roles, zero calls, everything intermediated by EY, so there is no champion, Economic Buyer, or pain owner to anchor a win.
EY reported an Argos leadership change and election-driven slowdown on 29 Jun 2026 after 'no update' on 16 Jun, so the initiative itself may be deprioritized under the new leadership.

The commercial substance is real: EY requested pricing 18 Feb, four pricing scenarios were shared 24 Apr, the amount rose from $100k to $350k on 8 May, and a business case was built with the EY team and Ronit through 26 May 2026.
Argos, via EY, voiced concrete product interest on 31 Mar 2026 in a spend-analysis demo and POC, the strongest buyer-stated signal on the deal.
142 days in Formal Evaluation (since 17 Feb 2026) with no demo delivered, no POC launched, and no movement toward a shortlist.

deal_momentum.text: No buyer-side action has ever been recorded; the last two partner touches were 'EY has no update' (16 Jun 2026) and EY citing an Argos leadership change and election slowdown (29 Jun 2026).
No future meeting is scheduled with EY or Argos, and no dated milestone exists between today and the 31 Dec close.
Karson Keogh's cadence is disciplined: dated Next Step entries every one to two weeks from 18 Feb through 29 Jun 2026, so the slowdown is explained rather than silent.
All motion since February has been rep-initiated follow-ups with EY; Argos has never initiated anything.

customer_commitment.text: Argos has committed nothing: no meeting attended, no inputs returned since EY's 18 Feb 2026 data request, no response to the 24 Apr pricing scenarios or the May business case.
The 31 Mar 2026 spend-analysis demo/POC interest (via EY) is the one buyer-stated commitment signal, but it is over three months old and never re-confirmed.

deal_risk.text: Political and leadership risk: the Argos leadership change and election slowdown reported 29 Jun 2026 can kill or indefinitely shelve the S2P initiative before Zycus ever meets the buyer.
Single-threaded through a passive partner: if EY cannot re-open Argos access, Zycus has no independent path into the account.
Forecast risk: Pipeline with a 31 Dec 2026 close is not defensible with zero buyer engagement; the honest category is Omitted until the initiative is re-validated.

customer_preference.evidence: No buyer-voiced preference exists anywhere. All positive signals (spend-analysis POC interest 31 Mar 2026, pricing engagement) are relayed by EY, and Argos has never spoken directly to Zycus.

business_case.evidence: A business case was actively built with the EY team and Ronit between 12 and 26 May 2026 (Next Step), but it is EY-framed with no Argos-confirmed metrics or pain owner, and Argos has not responded to it.

deal_movement.summary: The deal's last real movements are the commercial build-out in April-May 2026 and the amount increase to $350k; since mid-June the only movement is negative partner news. No stage, forecast, or close-date change since 17 Feb 2026.

day_summary.overall: The most recent real deal activity was 29 Jun 2026, when EY told Karson Keogh that Grupo Argos has had a leadership change and that the recent election has slowed the initiative. Nothing has moved since: no buyer response, no meeting booked, no deliverable in flight. The deal has been quiet for ten days on top of a buyer side that has never directly engaged.

items.summary: EY reported an Argos leadership change and said the recent election has slowed the initiative; no timeline or next step offered.

competitive_position.summary: No named vendor competitor appears in any field, note, or history across the deal's life. The real competition is Do Nothing: Grupo Argos deprioritizing the EY-led S2P transformation amid a leadership change and post-election uncertainty (EY, 29 Jun 2026). For this buyer, do-nothing means the new leadership simply never re-adopts a predecessor's procurement project, and the fully-priced $350k evaluation dies of inattention rather than losing to a rival.

competitors.name: Do Nothing
competitors.quote: EY mentioned that they had leadership change at Argos and due to the recent election things have slowed
competitors.how_we_win: Re-validate the initiative with Argos's new leadership through EY within weeks, framed as a low-commitment spend-analysis POC tied to the cost and compliance agenda a new leadership team must own, before the project is formally shelved.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Argos's expectations are known only through EY's asks, and Zycus has met each one: implementation and SaaS pricing data (requested 18 Feb 2026, delivered as four pricing scenarios 24 Apr), POC shape (asked 3 Mar, spend-analysis POC scoped by 31 Mar), and a business case (built with EY through 26 May). The unmet expectation is Argos's: none of these deliverables has received a buyer response.

best_practice_check.summary: The deal violated the partner-led playbook from the start: six months in, Zycus has never met the buyer. The levers now are requalification with the new leadership, direct Argos access, and converting the already-scoped spend-analysis POC into the re-entry vehicle.
flags: Requalify the initiative with Argos's new leadership before investing further: get EY to confirm within two weeks whether the S2P project survives the leadership change, and if EY cannot answer, treat the deal as Omitted (leadership change and election slowdown, EY 29 Jun 2026).

Break the single-thread through EY: after 175 days with zero Grupo Argos contacts mapped, secure at least one named Argos stakeholder (procurement or finance) and a direct conversation, using EY's own incentive to keep its implementation role.

Use the spend-analysis POC (Argos's own ask, 31 Mar 2026) as the restart wedge: a small, dated, low-commitment POC aimed at the new leadership's cost agenda is more likely to revive the deal than re-sending the $350k full-suite business case.

champion_strength.summary: No champion. Zero Grupo Argos contacts are mapped after 175 days, no internal advocate has ever been named, and the intermediary EY has moved from active (pricing and business case, Apr-May 2026) to passive ('no update' 16 Jun; leadership change and slowdown 29 Jun). The deal has no one inside the buyer pulling for it.
champion_strength.source: SF OpportunityContactRole (zero contacts); SF Next_Step__c 2026-06-16 and 2026-06-29

scope_change.detail: Amount raised from $100,000 to $350,000 on 8 May 2026 (field history, Karson Keogh) after commercial inputs and four pricing scenarios were worked with EY in April 2026, consistent with a full S2P suite scope of 13 products.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The proposed scope is the broadest AI footprint in its cohort (Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA, iLogix, iCompliance plus analytics), and the buyer's one stated product interest is AI-adjacent: a spend-analysis demo and POC (via EY, 31 Mar 2026). But AIS reads the buying style as procurement-rigor rather than AI-vision (AIS 5.0, Moderate), so AI is a supporting argument here, not the wedge.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AI Curious: real but narrow AI interest. The buyer-stated signal is a spend-analysis POC ask (31 Mar 2026, via EY), a discrete analytics use case, while the broader Agentic AI and Intake scope is Zycus-proposed rather than buyer-pulled, and AI importance is recorded as Neutral.

items.detail: Leadership change at Grupo Argos plus post-election uncertainty in Colombia has slowed the initiative and may reset its sponsorship entirely.
EY, the sole channel into Argos, went from driving commercials (Apr-May 2026) to 'no update' (16 Jun) and cannot currently provide access, stakeholders, or a timeline.
No Grupo Argos executive has ever been identified or engaged; with new leadership in place, Zycus has no relationship to defend the initiative internally.
31 Dec 2026 close requires demo/POC, selection, and contracting to all happen in under six months against a buyer that has not engaged since the deal was created.
items.source: SF CloseDate 2026-12-31; stage Formal Evaluation since 2026-02-17

confidence_signals.summary: Every confidence indicator points down: the buyer has never directly engaged, the partner has stopped producing updates, and the last news (29 Jun 2026) was an external blocker. The one genuine positive is that the slowdown is explained and dated rather than silent, and the commercial groundwork (pricing, business case) is finished and reusable the moment Argos re-engages.
